steps.title: Substitute and collect to standard form
steps.content: Set $y = x - c$ and $y = -4(x-8)^2$ equal: $x - c = -4(x-8)^2$. Expand and rearrange: $4x^2 - 63x + (256-c) = 0$.

steps.title: Discriminant shortcut for two distinct solutions
steps.content: Two distinct real intersections require $\Delta > 0$: $b^2 - 4ac = (-63)^2 - 4(4)(256-c) > 0$, which gives $3969 - 4096 + 16c > 0$, so $c > 127/16 \approx 7.94$.

steps.title: Check choices and match
steps.content: Choice C gives $c = 127/16$ exactly — discriminant $= 0$, meaning only one intersection (tangent), not two distinct points. Choice D gives $c = 13 > 7.94$, so discriminant $> 0$: two distinct solutions. That matches choice D — D is correct.
